description: Evidence matrix table for MEFV ubiquitin ligase hypothesis
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib
import numpy as np

# Create evidence matrix as a table figure
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(18, 10))
ax.axis('off')

# Evidence matrix data
columns = ['Citation', 'Evidence\nType', 'Supports/\nRefutes', 'Claim Tested', 'Key Finding', 'Context', 'Confidence']
rows = [
    ['UniProt O15553\n(database)', 'Computational\n& curated', 'Refutes', 'MEFV has E3\nligase activity', 'No RING domain; Pyrin(PYD)\ndomain at N-terminus instead;\nno ubiquitin keywords', 'Human protein\nannotation', 'High'],
    ['InterPro\nIPR050143', 'Computational', 'Qualifies', 'MEFV is a TRIM\nfamily member', 'TRIM/RBCC family member\nbut lacks RING domain', 'Domain\nclassification', 'High'],
    ['PANTHER\nPTHR24103:SF606', 'Computational\n(phylogenetic)', 'Source of\nannotation', 'Family has E3\nligase activity', 'IBA transfer from family\nto RING-less member', 'Automated\nannotation', 'Low\n(over-annotation)'],
    ['PMID:26347139\nKimura 2015', 'Direct assay\n(autophagy)', 'Refutes\n(alternative)', 'TRIM20 function', 'Acts as autophagy receptor\nnot E3 ligase; targets\ninflammasome for degradation', 'Human cells;\nautophagy assay', 'High'],
    ['PMID:26043233\nWeinert 2015', 'Structural', 'Qualifies', 'TRIM20 domain\narchitecture', 'Crystal structure of CC/B30.2;\nno RING domain resolved\nor present', 'X-ray\ncrystallography', 'High'],
    ['PMID:16237670\nMeroni 2005', 'Review', 'Qualifies', 'TRIM proteins\nare E3 ligases', 'RING domain defines TRIM\nE3 ligase activity;\nMEFV lacks RING', 'TRIM family\nreview', 'High'],
    ['PMID:16529770\nMassiah 2006', 'Structural', 'Weakly\nsupports?', 'B-box might\nhave E3 activity', 'B-box1 fold resembles RING;\nsuggests POSSIBLE E3 activity\nbut untested', 'MID1 B-box1\nnot MEFV', 'Very Low\n(speculative)'],
    ['Sequence\nanalysis', 'Computational', 'Refutes', 'RING motif\nin sequence', 'No C3HC4/C3H2C3 RING\nmotif; 0 Cys in first\n100 residues', 'Full 781-aa\nsequence', 'High'],
]

table = ax.table(cellText=rows, colLabels=columns, cellLoc='center', loc='center',
                 colWidths=[0.12, 0.09, 0.08, 0.11, 0.22, 0.1, 0.1])

table.auto_set_font_size(False)
table.set_fontsize(7)
table.scale(1, 2.2)

# Color header
for j in range(len(columns)):
    table[(0, j)].set_facecolor('#2c3e50')
    table[(0, j)].set_text_props(color='white', fontweight='bold', fontsize=8)

# Color rows by supports/refutes
for i in range(1, len(rows)+1):
    verdict = rows[i-1][2]
    if 'Refutes' in verdict:
        for j in range(len(columns)):
            table[(i, j)].set_facecolor('#fadbd8')
    elif 'Qualifies' in verdict:
        for j in range(len(columns)):
            table[(i, j)].set_facecolor('#fdebd0')
    elif 'Source' in verdict:
        for j in range(len(columns)):
            table[(i, j)].set_facecolor('#d5f5e3')
    elif 'supports' in verdict.lower():
        for j in range(len(columns)):
            table[(i, j)].set_facecolor('#d6eaf8')

fig.suptitle('Evidence Matrix: Does MEFV have ubiquitin protein ligase activity (GO:0061630)?', 
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', y=0.97)
fig.text(0.5, 0.02, 'Red = Refutes | Orange = Qualifies | Green = Source of annotation | Blue = Weakly supports (speculative)', 
         ha='center', fontsize=9, style='italic')

plt.savefig('evidence_matrix.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Evidence matrix saved.")
